rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BOOKER T. FOGGY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reconsider Order; Confirming Sale Of Estate Real Property; Memorandum P/A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Joan Lynn Foggy's ("Movant") Motion to Reconsider Order, seeking reconsideration of the November 18, 2022 Order Denying Motion to Set Aside Real Estate Sale of Estate Real Property and Motion to Set Aside Order to Authorize the Private Sale of Estate Real Property. Orders Confirming Sale of Real Property for each of the Alabama Street properties at issue were entered on April 13, 2021. Movant filed Motion to Set Aside Real Estate Sale of Estate Real Property and Motion to Set Aside Order to Authorize the Private Sale of Estate Real Property over a year later, and after the Order for Final Distribution was entered on October 28, 2022. The Motion to Set Aside Real Estate Sale of Estate Real Property and Motion to Set Aside Order to Authorize the Private Sale of Estate Real Property sought relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b). The motion was heard on November 16, 2022. An order were entered on November 18, 2022, denying the motion on several grounds, including the motion being untimely under Code of Civil Procedure section 473. As the moving party on a motion for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, Movant has the jurisdictional burden of filing the required affidavit with the required elements and showing both (1) "new or different" facts, law, or circumstances warranting reconsideration of the order, and (2) a sufficient explanation for why the "new or different" information was not offered before the order issued. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1008; Pazderka v. Caballeros Dimas Alang, Inc. (1998) 62 Cal. App. 4th 658, 670.) The party moving for reconsideration must not only demonstrate the grounds for reconsideration, but also "show diligence with a satisfactory explanation for not presenting the new or different information earlier." (Even Zohar Constr. & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal. 4th 830, 833.) The party may not simply resubmit facts, circumstances, or law previously raised in the first motion and call them "new or different" for the purposes of section 1008. (See, Kerns v. CSE Ins. Grp. (2003) 106 Cal. App. 4th 368, 383.) Here, the Motion to Reconsider Order contains Movant's declaration signed under penalty of perjury, in compliance with Code of Civ. Proc. section 2015.5. However, the declaration does not include a statement of new or different facts, law, or circumstances warranting reconsideration of the order, as required by the statute. The facts, law or circumstances advanced in the Motion to Reconsider and the supporting declaration are substantively the same the facts, laws, and circumstances advanced in the Motion to Set Aside Real Estate Sale of Estate Real Property and Motion to Set Aside Order to Authorize the Private Sale of Estate Real Property. Further, even if the supporting declaration to this Motion to Reconsider stated new or different facts, law, or circumstance warranting reconsideration of the order, it does not include an explanation for why the "new or different" information was not offered before the order issued, as required by the statute. Movant has not met her burden under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008. Motion to Reconsider Order is DENIED. Administrator Bruce Foggy is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/14/2023. (1) The court had required final accounting for the former administrator; however, the successor administrator is accounting the entire accounting period, including the period for which he did not act as the personal rep. Appears the successor assumes the liability of the former personal representative by filing this petition. This petition should have been filed as a stand along petition as there is no pending petition to amend. The petitioner should have paid filing fee and obtained a new hearing date. (2) Please pay the filing fee in full before the next hearing. (3) Affidavits that petition alleges to file must be on file before the next hearing.

(2) Petition and proposed order to describe the proposed distribution in compliance with CRC 7.651. Also, the distribution needs to state the dollar amount (if it is in cash) the beneficiary will ultimately receive after the fees and the costs are fully paid. Supplemental documents must be filed two weeks prior to the next hearing.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KEVIN D. SPENCER AND ALINA A. SPENCER 2016 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Order Directing Recording Of Corrective Deed To Correct Legal Description Of Property Held In Trust; And To Confirm Record Title
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/4/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proof of service filed on 1/6/23 is not sufficient for a trust proceeding. The only correct form of notice for an 850 petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. Notice must also include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. This court cannot correct another court's order; if a nunc pro tunc order is sought, the petition should be filed with the court that issued the order to be amended. Contrary to what is alleged in paragraph 18, however, Exhibit D does not reflect Petitioner's intent to transfer title to this trust. The San Mateo court confirmed the property as an asset of the Moreen J. Spencer 2008 Trust. This does not, in fact, appear to be a petition that falls under Probate Code section 850(a)(3)(B). The property, incorrectly described, was already transferred to Kevin D. Spencer and Alina A. Spencer, as trustees of their 2016 Trust on 2/14/18. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/22/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MYRTLE KING REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 2, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Loma J. Hobsons'S Petition To Contest The Validity Of The Myrtle King Revocable Living Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petitioner needs to show 1. Notice of Hearing (JC Form DE-120) and a copy of Petition must be given to all persons who are entitled to in the manner Prob. Code 17203. Petitioner filed only Proof of Service By First Class Mail; however JC Form 120 must be used. Also, all heirs at law much be noticed in the manner provided in CCP 413.10 et seq. as Petitioner requests the trust assets to be distributed pursuant to intestate law. 2. Petition must suggest a proposed fiduciary with his/her written consent to act. 3. Proposed Order must be submitted prior to hearing. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
